macro_summary: Friday's May non-farm payrolls surged to 172,000, nearly double the 85,000 estimate, sending the 10-year yield up 6bp to 4.54% and triggering a sharp tech sell-off. The S&P 500 fell 1.9%, with Broadcom's -6% post-earnings plunge and ongoing Hormuz supply fears deepening the decline. Next week's CPI report is the key binary for Fed hike expectations.
commodities_note: WTI fell 2.8% to $90.54 despite persistent Hormuz tensions; gold dropped 2.7% to $4,346.80 as real yields rose. VIX spiked 24% to 19.08 on the tech rout.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains stuck in NIM compression with US10Y at 4.54% and no MAS easing until October. All three tickers are extended and overbought â€” there is no entry in this group until yields break below 4.20% or local loan growth accelerates.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired â€” awaiting yield or policy relief.
singapore.key_development: OCBC's relative NIM resilience is eroding as US10Y ticks up, shrinking the intra-cluster performance gap.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster entered a sharp re-rating after Broadcom's $16B AI chip forecast miss, triggering a 7.9% SOXX plunge. The move reset several extended names â€” NVDA, TSM, LITE, and TSEM all upgraded to WATCH as they approach their 50-day averages â€” but the cluster-wide extension regime remains dominant, with 12 of 27 watchlist names hard-blocked on technical extension.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically resetting â€” the AI capex thesis remains intact, but the price correction is not yet deep enough for broad entry.
semiconductors.key_development: Friday's NFP surge to 172k drove yields higher, hitting growth stocks; the CPI report on June 10 is the next catalyst for rate expectations that could extend or reverse the semis correction.

big_tech.summary: BigTech held up better than semis, with GOOG flat while AMZN and MSFT slipped. MSFT's ACCUMULATE signal was downgraded to HOLD after the sell-off compressed R:R to 0.7:1. The upcoming WWDC on June 9 could provide a competitive read for GOOG and MSFT on-device AI.
big_tech.thesis_status: Cautious â€” MSFT's downgrade reflects the taper of near-term conviction, but Google's WATCH is the only actionable setup.
big_tech.key_development: Apple's WWDC next week may shift AI on-device narratives; watch GOOG's Gemini and MSFT's Copilot positioning.

interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if AI capex narratives re-ignite after CPI; watch Arista's Q2 earnings for cloud titan switch orders.

active_risks: Hormuz Strait remains contested â€” 95 days of paralysis; Iran's influence is institutionalizing, with a $10-20/bbl risk premium persisting.
US forced-labor tariffs on 60 economies (proposed June 3) threaten new trade war fronts; EU, China push back.
Fed rate-hike risk rises after strong NFP; Dallas Fed's Logan warned hikes may be needed.
AI capex sustainability questioned after Broadcom's forecast miss, raising risk of hyperscaler spending pullback.
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated oil and hawkish Fed drive risk-off: SPY -1.9%, semis crushed. Singapore banks under NIM pressure; PLTR finds some support from defense AI demand.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: May jobs report blew past forecasts at 172k, nearly doubling expectations, cementing rate-hike bets. Broadcom's $16B AI chip forecast miss triggered a semis sell-off, the largest single-day SOXX drop in months.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries until CPI clears or VIX subsides below 15.5; cash is a position in this risk-off tape.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive â€” risk-off triggered by hawkish jobs data and AI demand doubts.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 12 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, and the Broadcom-induced semis rout has not yet opened entry opportunities â€” wait for CPI to validate or reverse the rate-hike narrative.
